# Architecture Design Template

Template for `.planning/ARCHITECTURE.md` — prescriptive architecture produced by `/forge-architect`.

**Distinct from** `.planning/codebase/ARCHITECTURE.md` (auto-generated by /forge-map-codebase, descriptive).

---

## File Template

```markdown
---
status: pending_approval
style: [modular-monolith|microservices|serverless|event-driven|monolith]
approved_by: null
approved_date: null
modules: [list, of, module, names]
---

# System Architecture

## System Overview
[Architecture style, key characteristics, why this style was chosen.
Include: primary language/runtime, deployment target, scaling strategy.
Reference relevant requirements from REQUIREMENTS.md.]

## Module Boundaries
### [Module Name]
- **Purpose:** [what this module owns — single responsibility]
- **Public API:** [exported interfaces/functions — the contract other modules depend on]
- **Owns:** [data entities, routes, components this module is the sole authority over]
- **Dependencies:** [what it imports from other modules — list by module name]
- **Boundary rule:** [what external modules can/cannot import from here]

[Repeat for each module. Every module must appear in frontmatter `modules` list.]

## Data Model
```mermaid
erDiagram
    [Entity definitions and relationships]
```
[Entity descriptions, constraints, indexes.
Note which module owns each entity.
Specify soft-delete vs hard-delete policy.
Call out denormalization decisions with rationale.]

## Interface Contracts
### [API/Event/gRPC Contract]
- **Type:** REST | Event | gRPC | WebSocket
- **Path/Topic/Service:** [endpoint or event name]
- **Request/Payload:** [schema — include required vs optional fields]
- **Response/Result:** [schema — include envelope format]
- **Error format:** [structured error shape — code, message, details]
- **Auth:** [required | public — specify token type if required]
- **Rate limit:** [if applicable]
- **Idempotency:** [key strategy if applicable]

## Dependency Rules
[What can import what. Layer boundaries. Forbidden dependencies.
Express as explicit allow/deny rules.]

Examples:
- "UI layer must not import from database layer directly"
- "Shared utilities may be imported by any module"
- "Module-internal files must not be imported across module boundaries"
- "All cross-module communication goes through public API only"

## Technology Decisions
| Concern | Decision | Rationale |
|---------|----------|-----------|
| [e.g., ORM] | [e.g., Prisma 7] | [why this over alternatives] |
| [e.g., Auth] | [e.g., JWT + refresh tokens] | [why this pattern] |
| [e.g., State management] | [e.g., Zustand] | [why this over Redux/Context] |

## Non-Functional Requirements
| Metric | Target | Measurement |
|--------|--------|-------------|
| [e.g., Response time] | [e.g., <200ms p95] | [how to verify] |
| [e.g., Availability] | [e.g., 99.9%] | [monitoring approach] |
| [e.g., Max concurrent users] | [e.g., 1000] | [load test strategy] |

## File/Folder Structure
[Proposed directory layout — show top 2-3 levels.
Map each directory to its owning module.
Indicate shared directories explicitly.]

```
src/
  modules/
    auth/
      routes.ts
      service.ts
      entities/
    catalog/
      ...
  shared/
    utils/
    middleware/
```

## Architecture Decision Records

### ADR-001: [Title]
- **Context:** [why this decision was needed]
- **Decision:** [what was decided]
- **Rationale:** [why this option over alternatives considered]
- **Status:** Accepted

### ADR-002: [Title]
- **Context:** [why this decision was needed]
- **Decision:** [what was decided]
- **Rationale:** [why this option over alternatives considered]
- **Status:** Accepted
```

---

## Downstream Consumers

| Consumer | What It Uses | How |
|----------|-------------|-----|
| `forge-planner` | Module boundaries | File placement, dependency constraints |
| `forge-plan-checker` | All sections | Dimension 9 architectural compliance (BLOCKER) |
| `forge-verify` Layer 9 | Module boundaries + dependency rules | Post-execution fitness check |
| `forge-graph/module-detector` | Module list | Architecture-informed boundary detection |
| ADRs | All downstream agents | Become locked decisions |

## Usage Notes

- The `status` field gates downstream workflows: `pending_approval` blocks `/forge-plan-phase`.
- The `modules` list in frontmatter must exactly match the `### [Module Name]` headings.
- ADRs should only be created for decisions that are hard-to-reverse AND surprising AND involve trade-offs.
- Interface contracts here become the source of truth for `forge-verify` Layer 7 contract checks.
- Technology decisions here become locked — agents cannot contradict them without a new ADR.
